overview: This insect is known as the Target Longhorn Beetle (Hypsideroides junodi). It belongs to the family Cerambycidae and is recognized by its distinct "bullseye" pattern on its back. This species is typically found in southern Africa, including countries like South Africa. Like other longhorn beetles, it feeds on plant tissue. iNaturalist +4 5 sites Fit for a Tiny Robinhood | The Befuddled Loris

This insect is known as the Target Longhorn Beetle (Hypsideroides junodi).
It belongs to the family Cerambycidae and is recognized by its distinct "bullseye" pattern on its back.
This species is typically found in southern Africa, including countries like South Africa.
Like other longhorn beetles, it feeds on plant tissue.

Hypsideroides junodi is a species of beetle in the family Cerambycidae, and the only species in the genus Hypsideroides. It was described by Jordan in 1906.
